name: pdf-processing
description: Extract text and tables from PDF files, fill forms, merge documents. Use when working with PDF files or when the user mentions PDFs, forms, or document extraction.
capabilities: Extract text content from PDF pages using pdfplumber
Detect and extract tables from PDF documents automatically
Fill PDF form fields with values programmatically
Merge multiple PDF files into a single document
Split PDFs to extract specific pages or page ranges
Convert PDFs to images and perform OCR on scanned documents
author: davila7
securitySummary: Documentation-only skill providing Python code examples for legitimate PDF processing. All 42 static findings are FALSE POSITIVES: scanner misidentified markdown code fences as shell backticks and PDF form field values as cryptographic algorithms. Prior audit in skill-report.json confirmed safe status with no findings.
valueStatement: PDF documents contain valuable data but are difficult to process programmatically. This skill provides code patterns to extract text, tables, and form data from PDFs using Python libraries like pdfplumber and pypdf.
limitations: Cannot extract text from scanned PDFs without OCR (requires pytesseract)
Processing large PDFs may require memory optimization
Complex multi-layer PDFs may have limited extraction accuracy
Some encrypted or password-protected PDFs require decryption first

useCases.title: Extract tabular data from reports
useCases.description: Pull tables from financial reports and research papers for analysis in spreadsheets or databases.

useCases.title: Automate form completion
useCases.description: Fill out PDF forms with data from spreadsheets or databases for batch processing.

useCases.title: Build document pipelines
useCases.description: Create workflows to process incoming PDF documents for storage, analysis, or further processing.

promptTemplates.title: Basic text extraction
promptTemplates.scenario: Get text from a PDF file
promptTemplates.prompt: Extract all text from myfile.pdf and save it to output.txt

promptTemplates.title: Table extraction
promptTemplates.prompt: Find and extract all tables from report.pdf and save them to a CSV file

promptTemplates.title: Form filling
promptTemplates.prompt: Fill the fields in application_form.pdf with name=John Smith, email=john@email.com, address=123 Main St

promptTemplates.title: Document assembly
promptTemplates.prompt: Merge chapter1.pdf, chapter2.pdf, and chapter3.pdf into book.pdf

bestPractices: Test extraction on sample files before processing large batches
Validate extracted data matches the original PDF visually
Handle exceptions gracefully for corrupted or password-protected files
antiPatterns: Assuming all PDFs contain extractable text (scanned images need OCR)
Processing entire documents when only specific pages are needed
Skipping error handling for file access or format issues

faq.question: What PDF libraries does this skill support?
faq.answer: pdfplumber for text and table extraction, pypdf for manipulation, pdf2image for conversion, pytesseract for OCR.

faq.question: Can I extract text from scanned PDFs?
faq.answer: Yes, but you need pytesseract and Tesseract OCR installed. Regular pdfplumber cannot read scanned images.

faq.question: What formats can I export extracted data to?
faq.answer: Plain text, CSV, JSON, or directly to databases using Python libraries like pandas.

faq.question: Is my data safe when processing PDFs?
faq.answer: Yes, all processing happens locally on your machine. No data is sent to external services.

faq.question: Why does extraction fail on some PDFs?
faq.answer: Common causes include scanned images requiring OCR, password protection, corrupted files, or non-standard encoding.

faq.question: How does this compare to commercial PDF tools?
faq.answer: This provides code-based automation. Commercial tools offer GUIs but require manual steps for each document.
